title: Providing for congressional disapproval under chapter 8 of title 5, United States Code, of the rule submitted by the Department of Energy relating to "Energy Conservation Program: Energy Conservation Standards for Walk-In Coolers and Walk-In Freezers".
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
plain_summary: Congress is using this resolution to cancel a new rule from the Department of Energy. The rule would have required businesses to use more energy-efficient walk-in coolers and freezers. The Department of Energy said these new standards would save the most energy possible while still being affordable and technically possible. However, Congress decided to reject this rule and prevent it from becoming law. This means businesses will not have to follow the new energy-saving requirements for their walk-in coolers and freezers.

sections.title: Joint Resolution
sections.text: Congress disapproves a rule made by the Department of Energy about energy saving requirements for walk-in coolers and walk-in freezers. The rule was published in the Federal Register (the government's official daily publication) on December twenty-third, two thousand and twenty-four. This resolution means the Department of Energy's rule will not take effect and has no legal power.
